title: 10 best herbal teas for eye bags
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye bags are infusions made from plants that can help reduce the appearance of eye bags and dark circles. They typically contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can soothe and tighten the skin around the eyes. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for eye bags include chamomile, green tea, and rosehip tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness, while green tea contains caffeine and antioxidants that can stimulate circulation and decrease fluid buildup. Rosehip tea is rich in vitamin C, which can help brighten and firm the skin. Drinking these herbal teas or using them as a compress can improve the appearance of eye bags, making people look more refreshed and well-rested. This can boost their confidence and self-esteem, and positively impact their social and professional interactions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with eye bags because its high antioxidant content helps to reduce puffiness and dark circles. The caffeine-free herbal infusion also contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, white tea's ability to improve blood circulation can help to depuff and brighten the under-eye area, leaving you with a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to puffy, dark circles under the eyes.
Catechins: The catechin compounds in white tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the delicate skin around the eyes.

Vasodilators: Some herbal ingredients found in white tea, such as rose petals or chamomile, have natural vasodilation properties that can help to increase blood flow and reduce fluid retention under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, they help to nourish and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves by plucking them from the tea plant during early morning hours.
Sort the harvested leaves into quality grades based on size shape and color to ensure uniformity.
Roll the sorted leaves using a mechanical or manual process to release enzymes and start oxidation process for 15-20 minutes.
Dry the rolled leaves in direct sunlight or using machines at temperatures below 180°f for 2-3 hours to stop oxidation.
Store dried tea leaves in air-tight containers to preserve flavor aroma and nutrients for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with eye bags because of its natural cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint helps to reduce puffiness and constrict blood vessels, which can make eyes look more tired and swollen. By drinking a soothing cup of peppermint tea, the coolness helps to calm and de-puff the skin around the eyes, leaving them looking brighter and more refreshed. This natural remedy is a simple and effective way to combat eye bags and achieve a more rested appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dark circles and puffiness.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce swelling and ease congestion under the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Vasoconstrictive Properties: The menthol in peppermint tea helps to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce fluid buildup and puffiness under the eyes, making eye bags appear less prominent.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are valued for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a great addition to eye bag teas to reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of peppermint contain menthol and other essential oils that can help to cool and relax the eyes, reducing redness and itchiness associated with eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves by cutting off top growth from healthy mint plants.
Rinse the harvested peppermint leaves with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves or two tablespoons of fresh peppermint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Add honey or sugar to taste and enjoy your soothing herbal peppermint tea within three days of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with eye bags because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes. The antioxidants present in the tea help to reduce puffiness and dark circles by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention. As a natural relaxant, chamomile also calms the nerves, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate eye bags.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: German Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, alleviating the appearance of eye bags.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in German Chamomile tea, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and dark circles under the eyes, leading to a more radiant and refreshed appearance.
Soothing and calming effects: German Chamomile's natural sedative properties help relax the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing tension and stress that can contribute to puffiness and eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of German chamomile are often used in herbal remedies due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which make them effective in soothing eye irritation.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have been traditionally used to make tea that helps to reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried chamomile flowers for optimal flavor and potency.
Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers for every 8 oz cup of boiling water.
Steep the chamomile flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool and steep for an additional 2-3 minutes before serving hot or cold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with eye bags because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce puffiness and soothe tired eyes. The antioxidants present in the tea also help to combat free radicals that can cause dark circles and discoloration around the eyes. Additionally, the calming effects of pot marigold tea can help to relax the eyes and surrounding skin, reducing the appearance of under-eye bags and leaving you with a brighter, more refreshed look.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, reduce puffiness and swelling in the under-eye area by calming irritated tissues.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich polyphenols in marigold tea help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye bag formation, promoting a smoother and more even skin texture.
Vasodilatory properties: Marigold tea's flavonoids also have a vasodilatory effect, which means they help dilate blood vessels under the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling in eye bags.

Bark: The bark of the pot marigold plant contains flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making it beneficial for soothing and calming the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and leaves in the morning.
Rinse the harvested material with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with eye bags because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and swelling. The tea's antioxidants also help to combat free radicals that can cause dark circles and discoloration under the eyes. Additionally, the tea's soothing effects calm irritated skin and reduce redness, leaving the area looking smoother and more refreshed. By drinking littleleaf linden teas regularly, you can say goodbye to tired-looking eyes and hello to a brighter, more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Linden tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, alleviating the appearance of eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: Linden tea's antioxidant content can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin around the eyes, leading to dark circles or puffiness.
Vasoconstrictive properties: The flavonoids present in Linden tea have been shown to constrict blood vessels, which can help reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes and alleviate eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the littleleaf linden plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.
Bark: The bark of the littleleaf linden tree contains flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce redness and inflammation around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature littleleaf linden trees during peak growth seasons for optimal flavor.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently shaking off excess moisture with a soft cloth or paper towels.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried littleleaf linden leaves per eight ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized teapot.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes in boiled water, allowing the flavors to fully infuse and blend together harmoniously.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining leaves and particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with eye bags because of its unique ability to reduce puffiness and inflammation. The tea's antioxidants, flavonoids, and polysaccharides work together to constrict blood vessels, which in turn reduces fluid retention under the eyes. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, helping to reduce discoloration and dark circles. As a result, maidenhair tree teas can help to leave you with smoother, brighter-looking under-eye area.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause puffiness and dark circles under the eyes, reducing their appearance.
Vasodilatory Properties: The tea's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve circulation around the eyes, which can reduce swelling and alleviate eye bag discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce puffiness by soothing irritated skin and reducing redness in the delicate eye area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles.
Bark: Containing tannins that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, making it a great ingredient to combat water retention.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in boiled water for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors and nutrients release.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup or teapot.
Add honey or other sweeteners to taste and stir well to combine with the tea.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving hot or refrigerating for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bilberry teas helps with eye bags because it is rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties constrict dilated blood vessels under the eyes, reducing puffiness and discoloration. Additionally, bilberry's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to dark circles and eye fatigue. By drinking bilberry teas regularly, you can enjoy a more rested and revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Bilberry tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the delicate skin under the eyes, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.
Vasodilatory properties: The flavonoids present in bilberry tea help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce swelling and congestion under the eyes that can cause eye bags.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Bilberry tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by allergies or sinus pressure, which can contribute to the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, bilberry roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of flavonoids, rhyzomes promote blood circulation and reduce puffiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried bilberry leaves vaccinium myrtillus for infusion.
Combine ingredients in a heat-resistant container with a lid.
Pour boiling water over the ingredients for steeping time.
Strain the tea using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Serve immediately and enjoy as a soothing herbal remedy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butcher's broom teas helps with eye bags because it contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called ruscogenin, which reduces swelling and puffiness. The tea's antioxidant properties also help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and bags. Additionally, butcher's broom has natural astringent properties that help to tighten and firm the skin, giving the area a more refreshed and revitalized look.
remedy_properties: Vasoprotective properties: Butcher's broom tea has been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate puffy eyes and dark circles by reducing fluid retention and promoting healthy blood flow to the delicate skin around the eyes.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in butcher's broom tea may help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which can cause eye bags and puffiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Butcher's broom tea has been found to possess anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes and surrounding skin, leading to a decrease in the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing puffiness and soothing irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Known for their ability to calm and cool the eyes, rhyzomes help reduce redness and itching associated with eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems weighing about one ounce per quart of water.
Combine the herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant container at a ratio of one teaspoon to eight ounces.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has cooled down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids.
Enjoy the warm and soothing tea immediately after preparation or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with eye bags because it contains antioxidants that reduce puffiness and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes. The herbal properties also help to constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing the appearance of dark circles and bags. Additionally, eyebright tea's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm the skin, leaving it feeling refreshed and revitalized. With regular consumption, eye bags begin to fade, revealing a brighter, more radiant complexion around the eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, alleviating eye bag discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Eyebright tea combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye bag formation, promoting healthy skin and reducing signs of aging.
Decongestant properties: Eyebright's decongestant compounds help reduce fluid retention under the eyes, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the eyebright plant are used in medicinal teas due to their ability to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Seeds: Eyebright seeds are employed in tea blends because they contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate redness, itching, and irritation of the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers from the garden or store dried herb in airtight containers.
Measure one teaspoon of dried eyebright herb or two teaspoons of fresh leaves into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Pour boiling water over the herb, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids immediately.
Enjoy your eyebright tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, within 20-30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the delicate skin under the eyes. The antioxidants present in plantain also help to reduce puffiness and dark circles by improving circulation and reducing fluid retention. Additionally, the soothing effects of plantain can help to reduce stress and fatigue, common causes of eye bags. By drinking plantain tea regularly, you can say goodbye to puffy, tired-looking eyes and hello to a brighter, more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea contains flavonoids and polyphenols that help reduce inflammation and swelling under the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.
Antioxidant properties: Plantain tea's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting healthier and smoother-looking skin.

Astringent and vasoconstrictive properties: The astringent compounds in plantain tea help tighten and constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation and puffiness under the eyes, while also helping to reduce the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots are rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and irritation associated with eye bags.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant contains compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantain leaves and rinse them thoroughly with clean water.
Chop the fresh plantain leaves into small pieces to release their active compounds.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped plantain leaves in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool down and enjoy it as is or add honey or lemon to taste.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye bags with herbal teas is by using chamomile or green tea bags. After steeping the tea bags in hot water, let them cool down and then place them on your closed eyes for about 15 minutes. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in these teas can help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around your eyes. Do this routine regularly for best results. However, keep in mind that while herbal teas can alleviate symptoms, persistent eye bags might be a sign of underlying health issues. Consult a healthcare professional if you have concerns.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye bags that are treated with herbal teas include dark circles and puffy eyes. Certain herbal teas, such as chamomile and lavender, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. These teas can also help improve circulation, which can reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, the antioxidants found in some herbal teas, such as green tea, can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. To use herbal teas for treating eye bags and related ailments, soak a clean cloth in cooled tea and apply it to the affected area for 10-15 minutes.
